benchmark_name: C5 Python Kafka Consumer — Pre-Migration Baseline

methodology.note: Normalization-only — no ML inference, no live Kafka. Measures the Python EventNormalizer at 10K TPS. Full end-to-end latency (with Kafka I/O, ML inference, Redis) should be measured separately in canary.
methodology.environment: Ubuntu 22.04, Python 3.11, single-process, no GIL contention

gc_notes: Python CPython GIL pauses observed under multi-threaded workloads (not captured in single-threaded harness). confluent-kafka-python librdkafka I/O threads release GIL; event loop pauses observed at p99+.
known_bottlenecks: asyncio event loop: next-tick delivery latency ~50-200μs under high TPS
Python JSON decoder: ~5μs per message vs sub-μs in Go/Rust
GIL contention in multi-worker deployments limits true concurrency
fastavro: not yet integrated — current production uses JSON (slower than Avro)
